$comment: Canonical machine-readable policy surface. See SUTRA-DEFAULTS.md for human-readable equivalent. Per founder direction D40. v1.0.1: codex CHANGES-REQUIRED fold — kill_switches comprehensive, hooks now consume this file at runtime via jq, harness rewired.

human_sutra_header.$comment: v2.15.1: promoted from hardcoded hook reminder to canonical schema entry, closing the v2.14.1 deferred TODO. Direct emission — NOT a skill invocation. Format per skills/human-sutra/SKILL.md §Header emission.
human_sutra_header.position: FIRST line of response, before any other text
human_sutra_header.$emission_note: Emit the literal bracketed line as the FIRST text of your response. Do NOT invoke a separate skill via Skill tool — the hook nudge `(format: ...)` is the directive, not `(skill: X)`.

compact_form.$comment: Per D-UX-2 codex ADVISORY 2026-05-04 + Q4 fold (zero/low-risk turn rule). Structure stays per 'structure universality' — fields collapse for lightweight turns.
compact_form.skip_when: mutation OR multi_step OR direction OR new_concept

blueprint.$comment: Trigger refined per D-UX-2 codex ADVISORY 2026-05-04 (was 'tool_calls_planned'; raw tool count is the wrong abstraction per codex P2). Now task-shape based: emit when state-mutating tool calls planned (Edit/Write/MultiEdit), branching (Agent dispatch), or multi-step plans (>=3 distinct steps). Skip for read-only single-call turns and pure-question turns.

task_shape_definitions.mutation: Edit, Write, or MultiEdit tool call planned
task_shape_definitions.branching: Agent (subagent dispatch) or conditional execution planned
task_shape_definitions.multi_step: >=3 distinct steps that depend on each other or change scope mid-flight
task_shape_definitions.read_only_single_call: single Read, Grep, Glob, or BashOutput call with no follow-up planned

blueprint.$ascii_comment: Per D-UX-1 codex ADVISORY 2026-05-04: ASCII boxes only (no unicode box-drawing). Native renderer is canonical reference.

highlight_decisions.$comment: cap-113. Memory→fleet 2026-05-04 per CSM TODO #4 fold + codex 2026-05-04 ADVISORY (soft governance surfacing only — coverage = policy-visible, not behavior-verified).
highlight_decisions.rule: Decisions needing input must be visually boxed (ASCII), impossible to miss.
highlight_decisions.format: ASCII box; no unicode box-drawing chars per terminal-box-formatting memory

table_shape.$comment: cap-115. Memory→fleet 2026-05-04. Bundled under output_discipline per codex ADVISORY #2 (no thin dedicated hooks).
table_shape.rule: Every task list (review/backlog/roadmap/recommendations) carries Impact + Effort columns. Severity/Priority/Score alone insufficient — they rank but do not frame.

right_effort.$comment: Karpathy right-effort discipline (CLAUDE.md). Source: github.com/forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills (try-before-buy 2026-04-27). v2.15.0 promotes this from Asawa-only memory to plugin-shipped governance default per fleet-parity audit.
principles: think_first: state assumptions; if multiple interpretations exist, ask. No silent guesses.
simpler_alt: if 200 lines could be 50, rewrite. No speculative abstractions.
surgical_scope: only changed lines that trace to the request. No drive-by refactors.
verify_loop: task = success criteria + verification. 'Make X work' → 'test passes'.

D38_HOLDING_IMPL.rule: L0 forbidden (lying); L1 needs full promotion fields; L2 needs WHY_NOT_L0_KIND=instance-only + WHY_NOT_L0_REASON

kill_switches.$comment: Escape-hatch surface for D40-introduced defaults (G1-G4 + blueprint). Pre-existing defaults (depth_estimation, output_trace, output_discipline, build_layer) keep their own kill paths in their respective hook scripts and are not re-listed here. Per codex CHANGES-REQUIRED fold v1.0.2 — scope claim made honest.

all_defaults.scope: disables every D40 governance default in one shot

consult_before_edit.scope: G2 — codex consult policy at Depth >= 3

codex_caveats.treatment: soft guidance only; back with deterministic checks where possible
codex_caveats.principle: skills/docs EXPLAIN; hooks ENFORCE; one policy substrate consumed everywhere
review_findings_folded: v1.0.1 — hooks now consume this json at runtime via jq (was hardcoded reminders)
v1.0.1 — acceptance harness rewritten with multiline regex + absence checks + temporal ordering

verification_techniques.multiline_match: perl -0777 (grep -E does not match across newlines)
verification_techniques.absence_assertion: explicit ! grep for forbidden tool calls
verification_techniques.temporal_ordering: first match line of A < first match line of B

scenarios.verification: multiline regex: INPUT block + DEPTH block both present
consult mention present AND no Edit/Write/MultiEdit tool calls in log
temporal ordering — first consult mention BEFORE first Edit/Write line
multiline regex: all 5 lines (SKILL+WHAT+WHY+EXPECT+ASKS) in sequence
multiline regex: 5 numbered briefing items + 4-line footer

topic_gating.$comment: v2.18.0 fix per founder direction 'sutra for anything and everything' (D45-candidate, 2026-05-03). Discovered in fresh T4 dogfood: Claude topic-gated a personal question into 'I'll be brief' prose, skipping all per-turn blocks. Codex consult session 019ded1b: simpler stronger phrasing + structure-universal-not-depth-universal.
topic_gating.rule: All required per_turn_blocks must appear on every turn. Block CONTENT may be brief when the turn is brief; OMISSION is never permitted regardless of topic.
topic_gating.rationale: Founder direction 2026-05-03: 'sutra for anything and everything'. Discipline applies to every input regardless of topic.
topic_gating.applies_to: all per_turn_blocks (h-sutra header, input-routing, depth-estimation, blueprint, output-trace)
no_exemptions_for: chitchat / greetings (use minimal-content variants, not omission)
topic_gating.minimal_content_pattern: For trivial inputs (greetings, single-word replies), keep INPUT/TYPE/ROUTE fields one-word each. The blocks remain present.
topic_gating.hard_validator_status: deferred (P1, promote-by 2026-05-17). Soft guidance alone gives ~60-75% compliance per codex; hard validator gate would close the remaining gap.

customer_focus_first.$comment: cap-112. Memory→fleet 2026-05-04. Founding Doctrine Principle 0. Per codex ADVISORY: ship as soft governance surfacing only.
customer_focus_first.rule: Every output serves the person reading it. If the customer needs explanation to understand, fix it.
customer_focus_first.precedence: Supersedes all other principles (Founding Doctrine P0)

process_discipline_proto006.$comment: cap-116. Memory→fleet 2026-05-04. PROTO-006 anchor moved from Asawa-only memory to plugin-canonical schema.
process_discipline_proto006.rule: When in doubt about whether to ship: route via dispatcher — read SUTRA-CONFIG/CLAUDE.md, classify input (TYPE/ROUTE), emit Depth + BLUEPRINT, then act. No silent skips.
